elements.sent-2411-001: Planning with partial observation is a central challenge in embodied AI.
elements.sent-2411-002: A majority of prior works have tackled this challenge by developing agents that physically explore their environment to update their beliefs about the world state.
elements.sent-2411-003: In contrast, humans can imagine unseen parts of the world through a mental exploration and revise their beliefs with imagined observations.
elements.sent-2411-004: Such updated beliefs can allow them to make more informed decisions, without necessitating the physical exploration of the world at all times.
elements.sent-2411-005: To achieve this human-like ability, we introduce the Generative World Explorer (Genex), an egocentric world exploration framework that allows an agent to mentally explore a large-scale 3D world (e.g., urban scenes) and acquire imagined observations to update its belief.
elements.sent-2411-006: This updated belief will then help the agent to make a more informed decision at the current step.
elements.sent-2411-007: To train Genex, we create a synthetic urban scene dataset, Genex-DB.
elements.sent-2411-008: Our experimental results demonstrate that (1) Genex can generate high-quality and consistent observations during long-horizon exploration of a large virtual physical world and (2) the beliefs updated with the generated observations can inform an existing decision-making model (e.g., an LLM agent) to make better plans.
elements.figure-2411-001: \begin{center} \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{pics/teaser.pdf} \end{center} \caption{We propose the Generative World Explorer Genex that allows an agent to imaginatively explore a large-scale 3D virtual world and acquire imagined observation to update its belief. In this example, agent 1 (sliver car) imaginatively explores to the perspective at the location of agent 2 (taxi) with Genex. The imagined observation at agent 2's position (including the explored ambulance) revises agent 1's belief and enables more informed decision making. } \label{fig:teaser}
elements.sent-2411-009: Humans navigate and interact with the three-dimensional world by perceiving their surroundings, taking actions, and engaging with others.
elements.sent-2411-010: Through these interactions, they form mental models to simulate the world \citep{johnson1983mental .}
elements.sent-2411-011: These models allow for internal representations of reality, aiding reasoning, problem-solving, and prediction through language and imagery.
elements.sent-2411-012: In parallel, this understanding of natural intelligence has inspired the development of artificial intelligence systems that create computational analogs of mental models~\citep{ha2018world, lecun2022path, diester2024internal .}
elements.sent-2411-013: These {\it world models (WMs)~\citep{ha2018world, lecun2022path aim to mimic human understanding and interaction by predicting future world states (e.g., the existence, properties and location of the objects in a scene) to help agents make informed decisions.}}
elements.sent-2411-014: Recently, generative vision models~\citep{ho2020denoising, sora, bai2024sequential have increased interest in developing world models for predictive simulation of the world~\citep{du2023video, yang2024learning, yang2024video, wang2024language .}}
elements.sent-2411-015: However, these works focus solely on state transition probabilities without explicitly modeling agents' observations and beliefs.
elements.sent-2411-016: Explicitly modeling observation and belief is crucial because we often deal with partially observable environments where the true world state is unknown.
elements.sent-2411-017: An embodied agent is inherently a POMDP agent~\citep{kaelbling1998planning : instead of full observation, the agent has only partial observations of the environment.}
elements.sent-2411-018: To make rational decisions, the agent must form a belief, an estimate of the environment it is currently in.
elements.sent-2411-019: This belief may be incomplete or biased, but it can be revised through incoming observations obtained by physically exploring the environment.
elements.sent-2411-020: Typically, in an unfamiliar environment, an embodied agent must acquire new observations through physical exploration to understand its surroundings, which is inevitably costly, unsafe, and time-consuming.
elements.sent-2411-021: However, if the agent can imagine hidden views by mentally simulating exploration, it can update its beliefs without physical effort.
elements.sent-2411-022: This enables the agent to take more informed actions and make more robust decisions.
elements.sent-2411-023: Consider the scenario in \autoref{fig:teaser , suppose you are approaching an intersection.}
elements.sent-2411-024: The light ahead is green, but you suddenly notice that the yellow taxi in front has come to an abrupt, unexpected stop.
elements.sent-2411-025: A surge of confusion and anxiety hits you, leaving you uncertain about the reason behind its halt.
elements.sent-2411-026: Physically investigating the situation would be unsafe and even impossible at that moment.
elements.sent-2411-027: However, by standing in the taxi's position in your own imagination and envisioning the surroundings from its perspective, you sense a possible motivation behind the taxi's puzzling behavior: perhaps an ambulance is approaching.
elements.sent-2411-028: Consequently, you clear the path for the emergency vehicle, a timely and decisive choice, thanks to your imagination.
elements.sent-2411-029: To build agents capable of imaginative exploration in a physical world, we propose Generative World Explorer (Genex) , a video generative model that conditions on the agent's current egocentric (first-person) view, incorporates intended movement direction as an action input, and generates future egocentric observation.
elements.sent-2411-030: Although prior works~\citep{tewari2023diffusion can render novel views of a scene based on 3D models~\citep{yu2021pixelnerf , the limited render distance and the limited field of view (FOV) constrain the range and coherence of the generated video.}}
elements.sent-2411-031: Fortunately, video generation offers the potential to extend the exploration range.
elements.sent-2411-032: To address the FOV constraint, we utilize panoramic representations to train our video diffusion models with spherical-consistent learning.
elements.sent-2411-033: As a result, the proposed Genex model achieves impressive generation quality while maintaining coherence and 3D consistency throughout long-distance exploration.
elements.sent-2411-034: Furthermore, the proposed Genex can be applied to the embodied decision making.
elements.sent-2411-035: With Genex, the agent is able to imagine hidden views via imaginatively exploration, and revise its belief.
elements.sent-2411-036: The revised belief allows the agent to take more informed actions.
elements.sent-2411-037: Technically, we define the agent's behavior as an extension of POMDP with imagination-driven belief revision.
elements.sent-2411-038: Notably, the proposed Genex can naturally be extended to multi-agent scenarios, where one agent can mentally navigate to the positions of other agents and update its own beliefs based on imagined beliefs of the other agents.
elements.sent-2411-039: In summary, our key contribution is three-fold:.
elements.sent-2411-040: We introduce Genex, a novel framework that enables agents to imaginatively explore the world with high generation quality and exploration consistency.
elements.sent-2411-041: We present one of the first approaches to integrate generative video into the partially observable decision process by introducing the imagination-driven belief revision.
elements.sent-2411-042: We highlight the compelling applications of Genex, including multi-agent decision-making.

elements.sent-2411-173: When an LLM agent converts its view into a text description and relies solely on language-based commonsense reasoning, it tends to make incorrect inferences due to the lack of spatial context.
elements.sent-2411-174: This highlights the importance of integrating imagination with visual data to enhance the accuracy and reliability of the agent's decision-making processes.
elements.sent-2411-175: Genex has potential to enhance cognitive abilities for humans.
elements.sent-2411-176: Human performance results reveal several key insights.
elements.sent-2411-177: First, individuals using both visual and textual information achieve significantly higher decision accuracy compared to those relying solely on text.
elements.sent-2411-178: This indicates that multimodal inputs enhance reasoning.
elements.sent-2411-179: Secondly, when provided with imagined videos generated by Genex, humans make even more accurate and informed decisions than in the conventional image-only setting, especially in multi-agent scenarios that require advanced spatial reasoning.
elements.sent-2411-180: These findings demonstrate Genex's potential to enhance cognitive abilities for effective social collaboration and situational awareness.
elements.sent-2411-181: We introduced the Generative World Explorer (Genex), a novel video generation model that enables embodied agents to imaginatively explore large-scale 3D environments and update their beliefs without physical movement.
elements.sent-2411-182: By employing spherical-consistent learning, Genex generates high-quality and coherent videos during extended exploration.
elements.sent-2411-183: Additionally, we present one of the first methods to integrate generative video into the partially observable decision-making process through imagination-driven belief revision.
elements.sent-2411-184: Our experiments show that these imagined observations significantly enhance decision-making, allowing agents to create more informed and effective plans.
elements.sent-2411-185: Furthermore, Genex’s framework supports multi-agent interactions, paving the way for more advanced and cooperative AI systems.
elements.sent-2411-186: This work marks a significant advancement toward achieving human-like intelligence in embodied AI.. \bibliographystyle{iclr2025_conference .}

elements.sent-2411-187: An Equirectangular Panorama Image captures all perspectives from an egocentric viewpoint into a 2D image.
elements.sent-2411-188: Essentially, it represents a spherical coordinate system on a 2D grid.
elements.sent-2411-189: The ranges for these coordinates are $\phi \in [-\pi, \pi)$, $\theta \in [-\pi/2, \pi/2]$, and $r > 0$.
elements.sent-2411-190: Here, $u$ ranges from $0$ to $W-1$ and $v$ ranges from $0$ to $H-1$.
elements.sent-2411-191: This transformation ensures that the entire spherical surface is represented on the 2D panoramic image.
elements.sent-2411-192: Panorama effectively stores every perspective of the world from a single location.
elements.sent-2411-193: In our work, due to the nature of panoramic images, we are able to preserve the global context during spatial navigation.
elements.sent-2411-194: The spherical format allows various image processing tasks.
elements.sent-2411-195: For example, the image can be rotated by an arbitrary angle without any loss of information due to the spherical representation.
elements.sent-2411-196: Additionally, it can be broken down into cubemaps for 2D visualization, as shown in \autoref{fig:panorama_demo .}
elements.sent-2411-197: The rotation can be performed using the following formula: \begin{equation}\mathcal{T} (u, v, \Delta\phi, \Delta\theta) = f_{\mathcal{S} \to \mathcal{P}}\left(\mathcal{R}\left(f_{\mathcal{P} \to \mathcal{S}}(u, v), \Delta\phi, \Delta\theta\right)\right) \label{eq:composite_rotation_T_appendix}\end{equation} Where the rotation function \(\mathcal{R}\) is defined as: \begin{equation}\mathcal{R}(\phi, \theta, \Delta\phi, \Delta\theta) = \left(\phi + \Delta\phi \ (\text{mod } 2\pi), \theta + \Delta\theta \ (\text{mod } \pi)\right) \label{eq:spherical_rotation}\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-198: If there is no explicit input, both $\Delta\phi$ and $ \Delta\theta$ can be set to 0.

elements.sent-2411-272: Understanding, navigating, and exploring the 3D physical real world has long been a central challenge in the development of artificial intelligence.
elements.sent-2411-273: In this work, we take a step toward this goal by introducing GenEx, a system capable of planning complex embodied world exploration, guided by its generative imagination that forms expectations about the surrounding environments.
elements.sent-2411-274: GenEx generates high-quality, continuous 360-degree virtual environments, achieving high loop consistency and active 3D mapping over extended trajectories.
elements.sent-2411-275: Leveraging generative imagination, GPT-assisted agents can undertake complex embodied tasks, including goal-agnostic exploration and goal-driven navigation.
elements.sent-2411-276: Agents utilize imagined observations to update their beliefs, simulate potential outcomes, and enhance their decision-making.
elements.sent-2411-277: Training on the synthetic urban dataset GenEx-DB and evaluation on GenEx-EQA demonstrate that our approach significantly improves agents' planning capabilities, providing a transformative platform toward intelligent, imaginative embodied exploration.
elements.figure-2411-023: \begin{center} \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{pics/teaser-new.pdf} \end{center} \caption{GenEx explores an imaginative world, created from a single RGB image and brought to life as a generated video (top). With interactive and diverse generation, GenEx enables an agent to imaginatively explore a large-scale 3D world and acquire imagined observation to augment embodied intelligence (bottom). } \label{fig:teaser}
elements.sent-2411-278: To build agents capable of imaginative exploration in a physical world, we propose Generative World Explorer (GenEx) , a video generative model that conditions on the agent's current egocentric (first-person) view, incorporates intended movement direction as an action input, and generates future egocentric observation.
elements.sent-2411-279: As a result, the proposed GenEx model achieves impressive generation quality while maintaining coherence and 3D consistency throughout long-distance exploration.
elements.sent-2411-280: Furthermore, the proposed GenEx can be applied to the embodied decision making.
elements.sent-2411-281: With GenEx, the agent is able to imagine hidden views via imaginative exploration, and revise its belief.
elements.sent-2411-282: Notably, the proposed GenEx can naturally be extended to multi-agent scenarios, where one agent can mentally navigate to the positions of other agents and update its own beliefs based on imagined beliefs of the other agents.
elements.sent-2411-283: We introduce GenEx, a novel framework that enables agents to imaginatively explore the world with high generation quality and exploration consistency.

elements.figure-2411-025: \begin{center} \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{pics/architecture.pdf} \end{center} \caption{(a) Diffuser in GenEx, a spherical-consistent panoramic video generation model. During training, video $x_0$ is encoded into latent $z_0$ and noised to $z_t$. A conditioned UNet $\epsilon_\theta$ predicts and removes noise, resulting in $z'_0$ which is decoded to $x'_0$. The loss $\mathcal{L}_{scl}$ in (c) is combined with the original noise prediction loss. During inference, random noise is iteratively denoised to generate video $x'_0$ from an image panorama condition. (b) Left: Conversion between Polar and Cartesian coordinates. Right: Rotated spherical panorama can be converted to either 2D panorama or six-view images. (c) Spherical-consistent learning: we randomly sample camera orientation for edge consistency. } \label{fig:architecture}
